content_blocks.text:
2 classes and objects
this is a new chapter
27
1 was told there would be objects. In chapter 1, we put all of our code in 
the main() method. That’5 not exactly object-oriented. In fact, that’5 not object-oriented at 
all. Well, we did use a few objects, like the String arrays for the Phrase-0-Matic, but we didn’t 
actually develop any of our own object types. 50 now we’ve got to leave that procedural world 
behind, get the heck out of main(), and start making some objects of our own. We’11 look at 
what makes object-oriented (00) development in Java 50 much fun. We’11 look at the difference 
between a class and an object. We’11 look at how objects can give you a better life (at least the 
programming part of your life. Not much we can do about your fashion sense). Warning: once 
you get to Objectville, you might never go back. Send us a postcard.
 A Trip to Objectville
We’re going to 
Objectville! We’re 
leaving this dusty 01’ 
procedural town for good. 
1’11 send you a postcard.
